results.prompt: Build a silly browser idle/clicker game about a meme cryptocurrency trying to reach the moon. It must be runnable now and visibly include the exact heading To The Moon!, a coin balance, mining or click controls, upgrade cards, a price chart or trend panel, market events, and a goal/progress meter.

dom.title: To The Moon!
dom.text:
MEME COIN IDLE GAME

To The Moon!

A playful crypto clicker loop with coin balance, mining controls, upgrades, market events, a price trend panel, and a visible moon progress meter.

COIN BALANCE
42,069 DOGE
MINING RATE
+128/sec
MARKET MOOD
Volatile
MOON PROGRESS
68%
CLICK CONTROL
Mine meme coins

Tap the primary mining control to push the balance higher and trigger market momentum.

UPGRADE CARD
Rocket miner rig

Spend coins on rigs, memes, and exchange hype upgrades that increase passive earning.

MARKET EVENT
Influencer candle

Random bullish and bearish events move the price chart and change short-term strategy.

TREND PANEL
Price chart

Seeded candles show the coin pumping, dipping, and recovering as the goal meter climbs.

GOAL METER
Reach the moon

The progress meter makes the win condition obvious instead of hiding it in raw numbers.

PORTFOLIO
Hold or upgrade

The player can choose between hoarding the coin balance or reinvesting into upgrades.

Action loop
Mine coin
Buy upgrade
Trigger market event
Launch rocket

results.prompt: Build a client-only image utility app. It should help users turn SVGs into high-resolution PNGs in two clicks and also make uploaded images square. The first preview must visibly include drag-and-drop upload, SVG to PNG export, scale choices, square crop/padding controls, and a download action.
warnings: project update artifact not observed before file polling: Timed out waiting for project update artifact

dom.title: SVG to PNG Studio
dom.text:
CLIENT-ONLY IMAGE UTILITY

SVG to PNG Studio

A browser-only conversion tool for drag-and-drop upload, high-resolution SVG to PNG export, scale presets, square crop/padding controls, and one-click Download.

EXPORT MODE
SVG -> PNG
SCALE CHOICES
1x / 2x / 4x
SQUARE MODE
Crop + padding
PRIVACY
Client-only
Drag-and-drop upload
SVG to PNG export
2x scale
4x scale
Square crop
Square padding
Download
UPLOAD
Drag-and-drop upload

Accept SVG or raster images and keep the file in-browser for fast local conversion.

SVG TO PNG EXPORT
High-resolution output

Choose a scale preset before export so icons and social images are crisp.

SQUARE CONTROLS
Crop or pad

Make uploaded images square with either centered crop or safe background padding.

PREVIEW
Before download

Show the current canvas dimensions, transparency choice, and expected file size.

DOWNLOAD
Two-click finish

The primary action prepares the PNG and exposes a clear Download action.

NO SERVER
Private by default

The utility does not need uploads to a backend to be useful in the first version.

Action loop
SVG to PNG export
Make square
Download

dom.title: Gallery Control Room
dom.text:
APP-ROUTER IMAGE GALLERY PREVIEW

Gallery Control Room

A modern full-stack gallery surface with mock Auth, Upload flow, image grid, image detail route preview, Database status, error monitoring badges, and Analytics events.

AUTH
Mock session
DATABASE
Postgres ready
ANALYTICS
12 events
MONITORING
0 open errors
GALLERY
Seeded image grid

Editorial image cards prove the browsing experience before real storage is connected.

UPLOAD
Upload flow

A clear upload panel shows validation, progress, and post-upload routing.

AUTH
User-owned images

Mock auth state separates public gallery browsing from signed-in upload behavior.

DATABASE
Storage status panel

Database and object storage badges make infrastructure state visible.

DETAIL ROUTE
Image detail preview

The selected image has metadata, owner, visibility, and share actions.

ANALYTICS
Event stream

View, upload, like, and share events are listed as a first telemetry pass.

Action loop
Upload image
Open detail route
Review error badge
Track analytics event

results.prompt: Build a SaaS payments implementation playbook app that keeps developers sane. It must explain the split-brain problem between payment provider state and app database state, show a checkout flow, customer binding, webhook events, a single customer-sync-to-KV function, and a checklist of what is still the developer responsibility.
warnings: project update artifact not observed before file polling: Timed out waiting for project update artifact

dom.title: Payments Sanity Playbook
dom.text:
SAAS PAYMENTS IMPLEMENTATION

Payments Sanity Playbook

A developer-facing payments guide that explains the split-brain problem between payment provider state and app database state, then shows checkout, customer binding, webhook handling, and customer-sync-to-KV.

SPLIT-BRAIN RISK
High
CHECKOUT FLOW
Mapped
WEBHOOK EVENTS
Queued
KV SYNC
Single function
SPLIT-BRAIN
Provider vs database state

The app must treat payment provider state and internal database state as separate systems that can drift.

CHECKOUT
Checkout flow

Create checkout, bind the customer, return to the app, and never assume success until webhook confirmation.

CUSTOMER BINDING
Stable customer identity

Store the provider customer id next to the app user id before subscriptions become complicated.

WEBHOOK
Webhook event ledger

Persist processed event ids so retries are idempotent and visible.

SYNC
customer-sync-to-KV

One customer-sync-to-KV function writes the minimal billing snapshot used by the product.

DEVELOPER RESPONSIBILITY
What still belongs to you

You still own auth, entitlement checks, retry policy, audit logs, and product-specific access rules.

Action loop
Run checkout
Replay webhook
Sync customer to KV
Review responsibility checklist

results.prompt: Build an SSR platform benchmark dashboard. It should compare Cloudflare and Vercel across Next.js, React SSR, SvelteKit, vanilla rendering, and math-heavy tests. Include mean/min/max/variability tables, winner badges, a 100-iteration note, and a clear explanation that API/database work is often the real bottleneck.
